message:
fix(text,widgets,runtime): atomic Replace undo, table filtered selection, progress bar overflow, and observable borrow safety

**Text Editor — Atomic Replace operation (editor.rs, +50/-30 lines)**
- Added EditOp::Replace variant that stores both deleted and inserted text,
  enabling single-step undo/redo for selection replacement operations
- insert_text() with active selection now produces one Replace undo entry
  instead of separate Delete+Insert entries, fixing the 2-undo regression
- Extracted extract_selection() helper that removes selected text and returns
  the deleted content without pushing to undo stack (used by Replace path)
- delete_selection_inner() now delegates to extract_selection() + undo push
- Updated undo/redo apply logic to handle Replace by removing deleted text
  and inserting replacement at the same byte offset
- Test updated: selection-replace now requires 1 undo (not 2) to restore

**Table Widget — Filtered selection correctness (table.rs)**
- Fixed selection tracking to use original row indices (row_idx) instead of
  display indices when filtering is active, so is_selected/is_hovered
  comparisons match the actual data item, not the filtered position
- Selection validation now checks if selected index exists in current
  display_indices (contains check) rather than just bounds-clamping
- Out-of-filter selection falls back to first visible row instead of last
- Hit region registration uses row_idx so click handlers receive the
  correct data item index regardless of filter state
- Updated test name and assertion to reflect new first-row fallback behavior

**Progress MiniBar — width overflow guard (progress.rs)**
- Fixed potential underflow when area.width <= percent_width: now sets
  bar_width to 0 instead of computing with a potentially-zero available width
  and leaving render_percent=false with stale bar_width

**Observable — borrow safety during callbacks (observable.rs)**
- Changed notify_callbacks to hold the RefCell borrow during callback
  execution instead of cloning the value, preventing re-entrant mutations
  from observing stale state during the notification pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
